fields.description:
# Compaction: rename folded events to `*.retired` (invariant I1)

## Why
Compaction currently hard-deletes the event files it folds (`compact.py:140-144` `os.remove`). A
delete/add reconciliation (a rebase — the RC1 class) can then resurrect a folded source event and
produce `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`. `docs/concurrency.md` invariant I1 already SAYS compaction must
rename folded files to `*.retired` (git unions adds/removes) — but the code does not. Closing this
doc<->code gap is defense-in-depth and a prerequisite for A2's rebuild backstop and A3's remediation.

## What
Replace the hard delete with a rename to `*.retired`, and make retired files invisible to replay/fsck.
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py:140-144` — `os.remove(fp)` -> rename `<name>` to `<name>.retired`.
- `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` — confirm the `*.json` glob excludes `*.retired`.
- `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` — confirm `.retired` is skipped; `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` must not fire on a retired-but-present source file.
- `docs/concurrency.md` (I1) — align the doc with the code.

## Scope
Compaction + reducer/fsck exclusion + the I1 doc only. Does NOT change replay ordering (that is A2)
and does NOT remediate the already-corrupted live store (that is A3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Folded source files are renamed to `*.retired`, never `os.remove`d.
- [ ] Retired files are excluded from reducer replay and from fsck loops.
- [ ] `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` no longer fires when a folded file was retired (not deleted).
- [ ] Two-clone merge+rebase resurrection regression test: RED before, GREEN after.
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` I1 and the compaction code agree.
